title: How to Fix Audio Not Working on Linux
description: Troubleshoot and fix sound issues on Linux — from wrong output devices to PipeWire and PulseAudio configuration.

steps.title: Check the obvious things first
steps.content:
Before diving into terminal commands, check the basics:

- Is the volume turned up in the system tray?
- Is the correct output device selected (speakers vs HDMI vs Bluetooth)?
- Is the app itself muted?

Open **Settings → Sound** and make sure the right output device is active and the volume slider is up.

steps.title: Check which audio system you're using
steps.content: Modern Linux uses either **PipeWire** (newer, default on Ubuntu 24.04+, Fedora) or **PulseAudio** (older but still common). Check which one is running.

steps.title: List available audio devices
steps.content: See all detected sound cards and output devices. If your expected device isn't listed, it might be a driver issue.

steps.title: Set the correct default output
steps.content: If the wrong device is selected, switch to the correct one. First list the sinks to find the name, then set it as default.
steps.code:
pactl list sinks short
pactl set-default-sink <sink-name>

steps.title: Restart the audio service
steps.content: Sometimes the audio server just needs a restart. This kills and restarts the audio process.
steps.code:
# For PipeWire:
systemctl --user restart pipewire pipewire-pulse

# For PulseAudio:
pulseaudio -k
pulseaudio --start

steps.title: Fix HDMI audio with NVIDIA
steps.content: A common issue: HDMI audio not working when using NVIDIA drivers. The NVIDIA driver sometimes doesn't expose the HDMI audio device properly. Install the ALSA NVIDIA plugin and reboot.
steps.code:
sudo apt install libasound2-plugins
sudo reboot

steps.title: Install PipeWire (if using old PulseAudio)
steps.content: If you're on an older system with PulseAudio and experiencing issues, upgrading to PipeWire often fixes them. PipeWire is a drop-in replacement with better Bluetooth audio, lower latency, and fewer bugs.
steps.code:
sudo apt install pipewire pipewire-pulse wireplumber
sudo reboot
steps.warning: This replaces PulseAudio with PipeWire. It's generally safe, but make sure to reboot after installing.

seo.title: Fix Audio Not Working on Linux — PipeWire & PulseAudio Guide
seo.description: No sound on Linux? Step-by-step guide to fixing audio issues — wrong output device, PipeWire/PulseAudio restart, HDMI audio, and more.
